mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE < 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that lifts corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in the industrial production index reflects immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predicting 5-day returns during recovery phases, or positive returns dominate during production declines.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year CPI increase surpassing 3.0% triggers expectations of restrictive monetary policy that compresses equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered months over a 10-year sample, or the threshold shows no predictive edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or payroll growth shows no correlation with quarterly equity returns after controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand despite macro expansion, signaling earnings revisions that drive negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative out-of-sample R-squared, or forward returns remain positive when GDP rises and retail sales fall.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production increase exceeding 0.5% during a monetary tightening cycle confirms resilient supply-side capacity that supports equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening periods, or the predictive coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in regime-filtered samples.
